title: Respectful vs Humble Writing
learningGoal: The learner will construct sentences distinguishing between the actions of a superior (requiring Sonkeigo) and their own actions (requiring Kenjougo).
instructions: Write four Japanese sentences based on the English prompts. Select the correct Keigo verb based on whether the subject is the Boss/Customer, or You.
writingPrompt: Translate: 1) The president will go. 2) I will go. 3) The customer ate. 4) I ate (received).
promptGoal: To construct grammatically accurate sentences applying Sonkeigo and Kenjougo appropriately.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: しゃちょうは いらっしゃいます。わたくしが まいります。きゃくは めしあがりました。わたくしは いただきました。
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Shachou wa irasshaimasu. Watakushi ga mairimasu. Kyaku wa meshiagarimashita. Watakushi wa itadakimashita.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: The president will go. I will go. The customer ate. I ate.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect selection of Sonkeigo for superiors and Kenjougo for oneself.

evaluationCriteria.description: Did the learner write 'irasshaimasu' for the president?
Did the learner write 'mairimasu' for themselves?
Did the learner write 'meshiagarimashita' for the customer?
Did the learner write 'itadakimashita' for themselves?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: しゃちょうは いらっしゃいます。わたくしが まいります。きゃくは めしあがりました。わたくしは いただきました。
acceptableAlternates: しゃちょうが いらっしゃいます。わたくしは まいります。きゃくが めしあがりました。わたしは いただきました。

feedbackRules.onPass: Brilliant writing! You correctly applied the rules of respect.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the Keigo pairs. You CANNOT use 'meshiagarimasu' for yourself.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Make sure you used past tense for the second set of sentences ('meshiagarimashita').

hintText: If the sentence is about YOU, use Kenjougo (Mairu, Itadaku).
commonMistakes: Using standard forms ('ikimasu', 'tabemashita') instead of Keigo.
